---
layout: post
title: "The Rooms the Wildcard Named"
subtitle: "HTB Kobold, where a wildcard cert names the rooms it should have hidden, a developer inspector runs any command you hand it, and the box cuts its own phone line so you pin the answer to a tool's name tag instead"
date: 2026-07-13 12:00:00 +0000
description: "HTB Kobold walkthrough. A wildcard cert leaks the vhosts, an unauth MCP inspector spawns commands, and a reused password rides a root Docker manager to root. Sealed while the box is live."
image: /assets/og/kobold.png
tags: [hackthebox, writeup]
---

Kobold hands you the map before you ask for it. The certificate on the front door is a wildcard, and a wildcard is a name badge printed for a whole family, so it quietly admits the building has more rooms than the one you are standing in. You go find the relatives, and every one of them trusts something it should not. A developer inspector runs any command you hand it. A paste site executes a file anyone can overwrite. A password cut for a database also fits the door to the machine that runs the machines. And when the box cuts its own phone line, you stop asking it to speak and start pinning your answer to a name tag it was already willing to show you.

```
        k o b o l d            (easy · linux)
        =========================
        one cert, and it named every room.
        you knock the inspector.  it runs your words.
        you ask it to call home.   the line is dead.
                        |
                        v
        so you pin the answer to a tool's name tag
        and read it off the wall it already showed you.
                        |
                        v
        every lock here is somebody trusting a badge,
        a required file, or a copied password to be
        the harmless thing it claimed to be.
                                              . . .
```

The full chain, from the wildcard cert to root, is written up below in the usual detail. Because Kobold is **still active on Hack The Box**, that write-up is sealed. It unseals the moment the box retires. If you already hold the passphrase, the gate is right here.

<script type="application/json" id="lock-data">{"v":1,"iter":250000,"salt":"PKO7cnZB+++C7NQR+iRrLA==","iv":"rSg8pVWk3zljB7aV","ct":""}</script>

{% include locked-writeup.html %}

---

*HTB Kobold is an easy Linux box that reads as a lecture on exposed dev tools, shared writable mounts, and passwords copied from one lock to another, sealed here until the day it retires.*
